bullets.text: The US vice-president announced that Iran will allow nuclear inspectors back into the country following progress in initial talks between the two nations.
A technical accident at Qatar's main liquified natural gas processing site in Ras Laffan resulted in at least 13 deaths and dozens of injuries.
A new UN report indicates that the Myanmar army killed over 700 civilians, including 153 children, in six months last year.
The NSW budget includes a cap on Sydney road tolls at $50 per week, and the Treasurer has tempered expectations of a return to surplus.
A new poll indicates a significant decline in support for multiculturalism in Australia, with the share of people viewing cultural diversity positively dropping from 90% in 2024 to 73% in 2026.
Regional Australia is facing severe veterinarian shortages, leading to calls for more government support to attract and retain professionals in rural areas.
Former Federal Reserve Chair Alan Greenspan has died at the age of 100, prompting discussions about his legacy and his role during the financial crisis.
Carlyle Group Inc. is introducing a new framework to assess portfolio risk, aiming to incorporate the insurance implications of severe weather events into asset valuations.
The Economist reports that Russia's war economy, despite facing issues, is not on the verge of collapse, indicating Vladimir Putin's continued ability to fund military aggression.
